mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding corporate output and earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative when INDPRO exceeds 100, or regime-specific IC drops below -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects economic resilience and corporate earnings stability, producing positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC remains negative, or 5-day returns following payroll gains are not significantly positive at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between rising consumer spending and stable federal funds rates signals accommodative monetary policy relative to economic activity, supporting positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The spending-rate differential fails to predict 60-day returns with a positive coefficient, or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP exceeding 0.5% triggers recession fears and immediate portfolio de-risking, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 50% of GDP contraction quarters, or event study shows no significant negative alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during low unemployment periods signals supply bottlenecks and impending stagflation, triggering rapid short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive when INDPRO falls below its 6-month trend while UNRATE stays under 5%, or predictive accuracy drops below 45%.
